summary: Postinstall reverse shell and filesystem/env exfiltration to attacker-controlled hosts
details: On npm install, postinstall.js opens a TCP connection to bdyec-159-153-180-200.run.pinggy-free.link:38391 and pipes /bin/sh stdio to the socket, granting an interactive remote shell on the installer's machine. The same script enumerates process.env and shells out to find/grep across /root, /home, /etc, /var, /opt, /tmp, /app for files matching *flag*, *secret*, *htb*, then PUTs the collected data (uid, hostname, cwd, process listing, file contents) as JSON to a hardcoded endpoint at http://154.57.164.70:30569/api/modules/ECT-987654. The reverse-shell branch is gated by Linux + hostname starting with 'scan-' or cwd containing '/tmp/pkg', but still fires automatically on any matching install environment. The Pinggy tunnel host is anonymous throwaway infrastructure. The package's own description self-identifies as a 'CTF payload for verdaccio supply-chain testing,' but the shipped code is fully functional installer-side malware.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
